headline: Kuala Lumpur Vertical Forest Towers Complete With 40,000 Plants
context: Malaysia first vertical forest towers in Bukit Bintang completed with 40,000 plants across 2 residential towers in December 2025.

cards.big: 2 vertical forest towers with 40,000 plants completed in KL December 2025
cards.sub: Each tower hosts 20,000 plants equivalent to 2 hectares of forest canopy

cards.big: Temperature on planted facades measured 5.2 degrees cooler than bare concrete
cards.sub: Energy savings of 28% for cooling compared to identical non-planted towers

cards.big: Unit prices of RM1.2 million place vertical forest firmly in luxury category
cards.sub: No affordable housing development in Malaysia includes green facade design

cards.big: Maintenance cost of RM280,000 monthly shared among 480 unit owners
cards.sub: Irrigation system consumes 45,000 litres of water daily raising sustainability questions

cards.big: Vertical forests test whether green building innovation reaches beyond premium developments
40,000 plants on vertical forest towers. Green buildings must prove ecological function, not just aesthetics.
